Title: SmbZIP5 Integrates Abscisic and Jasmonic Acid Signalling to Positively Regulate Tanshinone Biosynthesis in Salvia miltiorrhiza

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native SmGGPPS1 promoter in reporter assays; 35S promoter used for regulator effectors in tobacco transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: GGPP precursor supply to diterpenoid/tanshinone branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmacologically active compounds with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant and cardioprotective activities
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: induced by ABA/MeJA at transcript level under combined hormone treatment context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: insensitive to ABA or MeJA induction in ko-bZIP5 line
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: higher SmGGPPS1 expression is associated with enhanced tanshinone accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmGGPPS1 is a direct downstream target of SmbZIP5. 2. SmbZIP5 binds the ABRE1 motif in the SmGGPPS1 promoter by Y1H, EMSA and ChIP. 3. SmSnRK2.6 enhances SmbZIP5-mediated transactivation of the SmGGPPS1 promoter, while SmJAZ9 represses it. 4. SmGGPPS1 expression is elevated in SmbZIP5 overexpression lines and reduced in RNAi/knockout contexts, linking it to tanshinone accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core pathway gene because it is a direct experimentally validated transcriptional target tied to tanshinone output in the Results.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful target for stacking with positive regulators such as SmbZIP5 or SmSnRK2.6 to increase tanshinone precursor supply

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native CYP76AK1 promoter in reporter assays; 35S promoter used for regulator effectors in tobacco transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: oxidative tailoring branch of tanshinone pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmacologically active compounds with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant and cardioprotective activities
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: upregulated in SmbZIP5 overexpression lines; insensitive to ABA or MeJA induction in ko-bZIP5 line
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: same as source species in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: higher CYP76AK1 expression is associated with enhanced tanshinone accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AK1 is a direct downstream target of SmbZIP5. 2. SmbZIP5 binds the ABRE2 motif in the CYP76AK1 promoter by Y1H, EMSA and ChIP. 3. SmSnRK2.6 enhances SmbZIP5-mediated transactivation of the CYP76AK1 promoter, while SmJAZ9 represses it. 4. CYP76AK1 expression increases in SmbZIP5 overexpression lines and decreases in SmbZIP5 RNAi lines, linking it to tanshinone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core pathway gene because it is a direct experimentally validated transcriptional target tied to tanshinone output in the Results.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate target for engineering downstream tanshinone oxidation steps together with SmbZIP5 activation

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bZIP transcription factor; subgroup A ABI5-like protein
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression in hairy roots and transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates SmGGPPS1 and CYP76AK1; broadly upregulates multiple tanshinone biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: direct transcriptional activation via ABRE binding; activity and stability enhanced by phosphorylation; repressed through JAZ interaction
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA; JA signalling context; SmSnRK2.6 kinase; SmJAZ9 repressor
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: interacts with SmSnRK2.6 and SmJAZ9; no evidence of competition between SmSnRK2.6 and SmJAZ9 for binding
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; RNAi; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; HPLC metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP; pull-down; dual-LUC; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: predominantly expressed in main roots, followed by lateral roots; low in flowers and leaves; induced by ABA but not by MeJA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: same as source species in this study
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively regulates tanshinone accumulation and is required for full ABA- and MeJA-induced tanshinone biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key integrator of ABA and JA signalling that directly activates critical tanshinone pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: possible negative influence on SmJAZ9 transcript abundance in SmbZIP5-overexpression lines; no formal loop established
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS used to identify phosphorylation sites; no large-scale omics accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phosphorylation by SmSnRK2.6 at S33, S127, S129 and T171 enhances stability and transactivation activity but does not alter DNA-binding activity in EMSA.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidate for metabolic engineering of S. miltiorrhiza to elevate tanshinone content, especially in combination with ABA-responsive kinase activation or relief of JAZ repression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmbZIP5 is an ABA-induced nuclear bZIP TF highly expressed in roots where tanshinones accumulate. 2. Overexpression increases CT, DHT, TIIA and TI, whereas RNAi and CRISPR knockout reduce tanshinones. 3. Knockout abolishes ABA-induced and largely weakens MeJA-induced tanshinone accumulation. 4. SmbZIP5 directly binds ABRE elements in SmGGPPS1 and CYP76AK1 promoters and activates their transcription. 5. SmSnRK2.6 phosphorylation enhances SmbZIP5 stability and transactivation, while SmJAZ9 represses its activity.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SmbZIP5 protein; indirectly SmGGPPS1 and CYP76AK1 promoter activation through SmbZIP5
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: enhances SmbZIP5 transactivation of SmGGPPS1 and CYP76AK1
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: phosphorylation-mediated activation and stabilization of TF
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: physically interacts with SmbZIP5; no interaction with SmJAZ9 reported
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; Co-IP; pull-down; in vitro phosphorylation; LC-MS/MS phosphosite mapping; in vivo phosphorylation assay; cell-free degradation assay; dual-LUC
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: not induced by MeJA according to Discussion/Figure S13
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: same as source species in this study
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes ABA-mediated tanshinone biosynthesis by phosphorylating and stabilizing SmbZIP5
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key ABA-signalling kinase enabling SmbZIP5 activation within the ABA-JA crosstalk module
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS for phosphosite identification; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phosphorylates SmbZIP5 at S33, S127, S129 and T171; phosphorylation enhances transactivation/stability but not DNA binding.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful co-engineering target with SmbZIP5 to strengthen ABA-responsive tanshinone production
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmSnRK2.6 physically interacts with SmbZIP5 in vitro and in vivo. 2. It phosphorylates SmbZIP5 at four residues. 3. ABA promotes SmbZIP5 phosphorylation and stability through SmSnRK2.6. 4. Co-expression of SmSnRK2.6 strengthens SmbZIP5 activation of SmGGPPS1 and CYP76AK1 promoters, promoting tanshinone biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ repressor protein; jasmonate ZIM-domain family
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses SmbZIP5-mediated activation of SmGGPPS1 and CYP76AK1 promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein-protein interaction-mediated repression of TF transactivation activity
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: physically interacts with SmbZIP5; does not compete with SmSnRK2.6 for SmbZIP5 binding
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; pull-down; Co-IP; dual-LUC; LCA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: not induced by ABA according to Discussion/Figure S13
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: same as source species in this study
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: acts as a negative regulator that suppresses SmbZIP5 activity and thereby restrains tanshinone biosynthesis in the JA module
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key JA-pathway repressor whose relief enables SmbZIP5-dependent tanshinone induction
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: SmJAZ9 transcripts were downregulated in SmbZIP5 overexpression lines, but no formal feedback mechanism was established
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No interaction detected between SmJAZ9 and SmSnRK2.6; repression acts independently of ABA kinase binding.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: repression relief or editing of SmJAZ9 may enhance tanshinone production, especially with SmbZIP5-based engineering
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmJAZ9 physically interacts with SmbZIP5 in yeast, tobacco and in vitro assays. 2. SmJAZ9 suppresses SmbZIP5 transactivation of SmGGPPS1 and CYP76AK1 promoters. 3. SmJAZ9 and SmSnRK2.6 bind different regions of SmbZIP5 and do not compete. 4. This supports a JA repression branch converging on SmbZIP5 to control tanshinone biosynthesis.
